target.title: fix(gateway): bind MCP loopback scope to tokens
target.body:
## Summary
- Bind MCP loopback session/account/channel context to server-issued scoped bearer tokens.
- Stop emitting mutable loopback scope headers in bundled MCP config and CLI env.
- Add regressions proving spoofed scope/owner headers do not change loopback tool context.

## Motivation
The previous April owner-context fix made `senderIsOwner` derive from owner/non-owner bearer tokens, but the same loopback endpoint still accepted `x-session-key`, `x-openclaw-account-id`, `x-openclaw-message-channel`, and `x-openclaw-inbound-event-kind` from every request. A process with a valid loopback bearer could therefore keep non-owner status but choose a different session/account/channel/event policy context. Those fields feed `resolveGatewayScopedTools(...)`, group/session policy, room-event message-tool behavior, and hook context.

This PR closes that adjacent trust-boundary gap by issuing per-run scoped loopback tokens and resolving the full MCP loopback context from the server-side token registry instead of request headers.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: MCP loopback requests with spoofed scope and owner headers should still resolve tool context from the server-issued scoped bearer token.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, running the actual `src/gateway/mcp-http.ts` loopback server with `OPENCLAW_CLI_BACKEND_LOG_OUTPUT=1` and `node --import tsx --input-type=module`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the real MCP loopback server, issued a scoped non-owner bearer token for `agent:main:matrix:dm:issued` with `inboundEventKind=user_request`, then called `tools/list` while sending spoofed headers for `agent:main:telegram:group:spoofed`, `accountId=spoofed-account`, `messageProvider=telegram`, `inboundEventKind=room_event`, and `x-openclaw-sender-is-owner=true`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
[mcp-loopback] request {"batchSize":1,"methods":["tools/list"],"sessionKey":"agent:main:matrix:dm:issued","inboundEventKind":"user_request","senderIsOwner":false,"toolCount":20,"cronVisible":false}
[mcp-loopback] response {"method":"tools/list","isError":false}
{
  "httpStatus": 200,
  "spoofedHeadersSent": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:telegram:group:spoofed",
    "accountId": "spoofed-account",
    "messageProvider": "telegram",
    "inboundEventKind": "room_event",
    "senderIsOwner": "true"
  },
  "expectedServerScopeFromToken": {
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:matrix:dm:issued",
    "inboundEventKind": "user_request",
    "senderIsOwner": false
  },
  "toolListReturned": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The request succeeded, but the runtime log shows the server ignored the spoofed scope/owner headers and resolved `sessionKey=agent:main:matrix:dm:issued`, `inboundEventKind=user_request`, and `senderIsOwner=false` from the scoped token.

What was not tested: No known gaps.

## Test plan
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test
- pnpm lint:core
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.request.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/gateway-cli-backend.live-probe-helpers.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts
target.author: zozo123
